claimText: Under the uniform asset classification system, if a single lender within a consortium designates a loan as a Non-performing Asset (NPA), all other co-lenders are mandated to adopt the same classification.
evidenceExcerpt: The uniform asset classification system mandates that if one lender in a consortium tags a loan as a Non-performing Asset (NPA), all other co-lenders must also classify it as an NPA.
distractorHooks: A majority of lenders in the consortium must agree to classify a loan as an NPA.
Only the lead bank's classification of an asset is binding on other lenders.
Co-lenders can independently classify the same loan asset.
The RBI must first approve the NPA classification before it is uniformly applied.
